metadata.title: Black Holes as Reverse TZP Processes
metadata.creator: Daniel Alexander Trawin

master_row.title: Black Holes as Reverse TZP Processes
master_row.canonical_statement: Black holes represent the reverse of TZP emergences: a collective distortion of the vacuum becomes so extreme that matter collapses back into the zero-point field, erasing local decoherence.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a symbolic expression relating Δ, M, V, S.
master_row.dictionary: Black holes represent the reverse of TZP emergences: a collective distortion of the vacuum becomes so extreme that matter collapses back into the zero‑point field, erasing local decoherence【952647870353647†L437-L459】.
